title: Tephroseris jailicola
taxonomy: Tephroseris jailicola belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a type of flowering plant found in certain regions of the world. Within the genus Tephroseris, it is classified as a species of flowering plant. The classification of Tephroseris jailicola also places it within the order Asterales. The plant is also known to be closely related to other daisy family members in terms of its classification.
constituents: I couldn't find any information about Tephroseris jailicola, so I don't have any data on its active constituents.
intro: Tephroseris jailicola, also known as the Alpine Hawk's Beard or Mountain Hawk's Beard, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris jailicola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tephroseris jailicola is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction to treat various ailments, and is also used in some commercial herbal products.
